intro: Vernonella luembensis, also known as Luembe's leaf, is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, Vernonella luembensis is used in folk medicine, and research has shown that it possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents of Vernonella luembensis include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as luembenol and vernol, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
